Print a Month

What is this?

We all have digital calendars these days. They’re on our phones, in our browsers, in our email software, in our operating systems. They’re everywhere, and that can be a problem.

Calendar fatigue can lead to updating the wrong one, or things getting out of sync, and family members forgetting about something they should be doing because it wasn’t on the right calendar. Another issue is not being able to see more than whatever fits on your screen at one time. Some definitely find it easier to keep up with everything that’s going on if they can see a few months at once, and even better, have them printed out and tacked up on the wall.

This is what “Print a Month” was created to do. It helps the person it was made for, and maybe it could help you too. It’s very simple, it does exactly what it says. It shows you a month, and you can print it. Then you can write whatever you want on it, you can stick up multiple months at once, whatever you need.

PRINTING TIPS:

  • Make sure “Print backgrounds” or equivalent is switched on
  • Make sure “Print headers and footers” or equivalent is switched off
  • Print at 100% scale

The above will ensure that your months all look consistent, and each one will fit on a single sheet of A4. Most months will require 5 rows, though a few will use 6 (and if February in a non-leap year begins on a Monday, it will use 4.)

© Clive Murray
